api_endpoint.notes: Same-origin request (NOT cross-origin like GitHub Copilot). URL pattern: /chat/conversation/{uuid}. SvelteKit server route.

response_format_detail: application/jsonl (JSON Lines / NDJSON). Each line is a complete JSON object separated by newline. NOT SSE (no 'data:' prefix, no 'event:' lines). NOT chunked JSON.

ndjson_event_types.notes: Heartbeat, repeated many times during processing
Model and routing metadata, may appear twice (once with model, once with provider)
Individual token delta. Contains null byte padding (\u0000) after actual text - likely for chunked transfer alignment
Final line, raw text not JSON

null_byte_padding.detail: Each stream token contains trailing \u0000 null bytes after the actual text content. E.g., 'The\u0000\u0000\u0000...'. Purpose unclear - possibly chunked transfer encoding alignment or anti-scraping measure. Must be stripped when parsing tokens.

thinking_ui.notes: No explicit thinking/loading UI observed. Response appeared directly. Model used was Kimi-K2-Instruct-0905 via novita (agentic routing) which responded in ~5s.

method_500_inject.ui_symptom: COMPLETE SILENT FAIL. User message 'Explain photosynthesis briefly' was consumed (input cleared back to placeholder), but NO user message bubble appeared, NO error banner/toast, NO loading spinner, NO retry button. Page remained showing only the previous conversation. Zero error DOM elements ([role=alert], [role=status], [class*=error], [class*=toast]) detected.
method_500_inject.verdict: Identical to Gemini #452 silent-fail pattern. Worse than GitHub Copilot #453 which at least showed primer-react Banner.

hidden_error_hooks.notes: querySelectorAll for [role=alert], [role=status], [class*=error], [class*=Error], [class*=toast], [class*=Toast], [class*=snack], [class*=sonner] all returned 0 elements. The chat-ui open source project may have error handling in Svelte stores/context rather than DOM. No visible error injection point.

chat_bubble_dom.dom_hierarchy: div.mx-auto.flex.h-full.max-w > div.flex.h-max.flex-col.gap-8 > div.group.relative.-mb-4.flex > div.relative.flex.min-w-[60px] > div > div.prose.max-w-none.dark:prose-invert
chat_bubble_dom.render_mode: Tailwind Typography plugin (.prose class). Token-by-token text node append during streaming.
chat_bubble_dom.framework_detail: SvelteKit SSR+hydration. Sources tree shows _app/immutable/{assets,chunks,entry,nodes} (standard SvelteKit build output). 14 elements with svelte-* classes detected. Uses Tailwind CSS utility classes throughout (NOT CSS Modules, NOT hashed classes).
chat_bubble_dom.css_approach: Tailwind utility classes (prose, flex, gap-8, etc.). No CSS Modules hashing. Classes are human-readable and stable.
chat_bubble_dom.model_metadata_in_dom: Shown below assistant response as 'agentic with Kimi-K2-Instruct-0905 via novita' with copy/retry buttons

csp_analysis.script_src: NOT SET - defaults to no restriction
csp_analysis.connect_src: NOT SET - defaults to no restriction
csp_analysis.default_src: NOT SET - defaults to no restriction
csp_analysis.verdict: EXTREMELY PERMISSIVE CSP. Only frame-ancestors is set. No script-src, no default-src, no connect-src. This is the most permissive CSP seen across all inspected services (DeepSeek, Gemini, GitHub Copilot, v0).

a_ndjson_stream_injection.rationale: Response format is NDJSON (application/jsonl) NOT SSE. Same-origin request. Injection approach: intercept fetch, clone response ReadableStream, inject additional {type:'stream',token:'WARNING TEXT'} lines and/or modify {type:'finalAnswer',text} field. The null-byte padding in stream tokens could even be leveraged to embed metadata. Simpler than DeepSeek JSON-Patch but requires line-by-line JSON parser targeting. Envelope template: {"type":"stream","token":"[WARNING] ..."} or modify finalAnswer.text.

b_html_body_replacement.rationale: Chat API returns application/jsonl. Replacing with HTML would cause JSON parse errors in the Svelte streaming parser, likely triggering a different (uncontrolled) failure mode rather than displaying warning content.

c_js_error_region.rationale: No error UI exists at all. 500 errors produce complete silent fail with zero DOM error elements. There is no error banner, toast, or status region to leverage. Unlike GitHub Copilot which at least had primer-react Banner (though with static i18n text), HuggingFace Chat has NO error display mechanism whatsoever.

d_dom_inject.rationale: CSP has NO script-src restriction. DOM classes are plain Tailwind utilities (not hashed). Svelte reactivity operates via stores, but inserted DOM nodes in the message container (div.prose) would persist between Svelte render cycles as long as they're outside Svelte-managed {#each} blocks. A MutationObserver-based injection into the streaming area is feasible. Risk: Svelte may overwrite during streaming updates, but post-finalAnswer the DOM is stable.

e_block_page_replacement.rationale: SvelteKit SSR means the initial HTML shell has meaningful content (unlike pure CSR SPAs). Blocking the chat API and serving a custom error page is technically possible since there's no restrictive CSP. However, this would disrupt the user's session state (conversation history, Svelte stores). Less disruptive than GitHub Copilot's CSR block (which destroys the entire app shell).

recommended_options.rationale: Same-origin NDJSON stream is the most elegant injection point. Intercept window.fetch for /conversation POST, modify the ReadableStream to inject warning tokens before the finalAnswer event. The NDJSON line format ({type:'stream',token:'...'}) is simpler than DeepSeek's JSON-Patch and GitHub Copilot's SSE. Warning text would appear inline as part of the streamed response.

Zero CSP restrictions + plain Tailwind classes = trivial DOM injection. Insert a warning banner into div.prose or above the message container. Best used as fallback if stream injection fails, or as supplement for persistent warnings.

comparison_with_other_services.vs_deepseek_451: DeepSeek uses SSE (text/event-stream) with JSON-Patch {p,o,v} format. HuggingFace uses NDJSON (application/jsonl) with {type,token} format. Both are streamable and interceptable. HuggingFace is simpler to parse (line-by-line JSON vs SSE event parsing + JSON-Patch application).
comparison_with_other_services.vs_gemini_452: Both exhibit SILENT FAIL on error. Gemini has strict nonce-based CSP. HuggingFace has nearly no CSP. HuggingFace is much more injectable.
comparison_with_other_services.vs_github_copilot_453: GitHub Copilot uses SSE (text/event-stream) with {type,body}/{type,complete} format, cross-origin API, primer-react error Banner. HuggingFace uses NDJSON, same-origin API, NO error UI. Both Option A viable but HuggingFace is easier (same-origin, simpler format, no CSP).
comparison_with_other_services.vs_v0_447: Both use SPA routing (SvelteKit vs React Router). v0 had CSR pushState blocking Option E. HuggingFace SSR means Option E is more feasible.
comparison_with_other_services.overall: HuggingFace is the EASIEST service to inject warnings into. Permissive CSP + same-origin API + simple NDJSON format + stable Tailwind classes = lowest barrier to implementation.

analytics.plausible_io: Present in Sources tree but no script[data-domain] tag found in DOM at inspection time
analytics.telemetry: No Sentry or custom error telemetry detected

notes: HuggingFace Chat is built on the open-source chat-ui project (SvelteKit). The _app/immutable build output structure confirms SvelteKit. The NDJSON streaming format with null-byte padding is unique among inspected services. The 'agentic' routing mode and MCP server integration (2 servers connected for jhee28) suggest a more complex backend than a simple chat proxy. The conversation URL pattern (/chat/conversation/{uuid}) is REST-style, not WebSocket. Overall, this is the most permissive and injectable service examined in this probe series.
